body.title: Investigate pytest runner hanging with no pipe output
body.result: pytest.ini timeout=30 + pytest-timeout dep. Root cause was hanging tests, not pipe buffering. /sprint test step now produces output in ~96s.
body.description:
During T-palace-ticket-design-rules sprint (2026-04-21), `python -m pytest tests/ -x -q 2>&1 | tail -12` was backgrounded and produced zero output across 60+ minutes — output file stayed at 0 bytes. The test suite is expected to complete in well under a minute when piped through `tail`. This was eventually killed.

Symptoms: no progress lines, no errors, no final summary reached stdout/the output file. Either pytest itself hung, or the `| tail -12` pipe buffered the whole stream and the process never got around to closing it, or the venv was initialized in a state that blocked.

Repro attempt: run `cd ~/TheIgors && source venv/bin/activate && IGOR_HOME_DB_URL=postgresql://igor:choose_a_password@127.0.0.1/Igor-wild-0001 python -m pytest tests/ -x -q` WITHOUT `| tail` and see if it completes. If yes: the tail pipe is the issue (buffering). Fix: adjust /sprint step 7 to redirect to a file and tail after, or use `stdbuf -oL` to force line-buffered.

Also check: did the earlier `python -m pytest ... | tail -10` (without env var) error-out and leave pytest's state/fixtures in an unknown state that blocked the second invocation?

test_plan: no automated test — this is a runner-fix ticket. Verification is: after fix, /sprint's test step consistently produces output within ~60s.

Scope: /sprint SKILL.md test step + possibly /sprint-batch test step. Does NOT touch actual pytest code or tests.
